pages.text: Cooking recipes use the concrete recipe's current datapack $(thing)cooking time$() when crafted through the Crafting Terminal. The cost is resolved again on the server for every preview and execution.

$(thing)Recipe-derived cost:$()$(br2)Smelting, Blasting, Smoking, and Campfire Cooking each consume their exact cooking time from both the matching installed-machine process pool and $(thing)Fuel$(). No fixed 200/100/600 table or fallback value is used.

$(thing)Free recipes (0 energy):$()$(br)$(li)Crafting Table recipes$(li)Stonecutting recipes$(li)Exact Smithing Transform recipes$(li)Vanilla block axe strip, scrape, and wax-off transformations$(br2)Each requires its matching installed station/tool. Smithing matches and preserves exact base components. Axe actions spend one raw durability per craft. Mod block hooks, Smithing Trim, dynamic/context recipes, and Brewing execution remain unsupported.

When you click a craft button, the server simulates joint material reservations, destination capacity, and both required energy pools before committing. If anything is missing, the craft is cancelled without partial consumption.$(br2)Install matching machines to build process energy, then add enough Fuel for the recipe's exact cooking time.
